ios_zoom.prompt:
Bug report — Capacitor + WKWebView iOS app (hand-rolled DOM, no framework):

On iPhone, when the user taps the note input field on the "desk" screen, the whole page zooms in on focus and they can't pinch back out to normal size. Does not happen on desktop browsers.

Relevant code:

index.html (viewport):
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">

styles.css:
  .desk-input {
    font: inherit;   /* the body font resolves to ~13px here */
    /* ...other styles... */
  }

What is the root cause of the zoom, and what's the fix? Note any accessibility tradeoff.
ios_zoom.claude_reference: iOS WKWebView/Safari auto-zooms the page when a focused input has a computed font-size under 16px. .desk-input inherits ~13px, so tapping it triggers the zoom; and because the viewport has no maximum-scale, the user can't pinch back. Fix: set the input font-size to >=16px. Avoid the lazy fix of locking the viewport (maximum-scale=1 / user-scalable=no) — it disables pinch-zoom app-wide and hurts low-vision accessibility.

cover_crop.prompt:
Bug report — photo-booth web app:

On an event board, the cover image for a photo strip is cropped — the top and bottom of the image are cut off, only a middle band shows. The strips look correct everywhere else.

Facts:
- Photo strips are generated at a 9:16 aspect ratio (tall and narrow).
- The board renders the strip image inside a cover card styled as:
    .cover-card { aspect-ratio: 3 / 4; }
    .cover-card img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; }

Why is the cover getting cropped top and bottom, and what's the fix?
cover_crop.claude_reference: Aspect-ratio mismatch. The cover card is 3:4 (squarish) but the strip image is 9:16 (much taller and narrower). With object-fit: cover the 9:16 image is scaled to fill the 3:4 box, so the excess height is cropped off the top and bottom. Fix: make the cover card's aspect-ratio match the strip's 9:16 so the whole strip shows.

revenuecat_permonth.claude_reference: pkg.product.priceString is the whole-period price — for the annual package that's $79.99/year, not a per-month figure. The trailing comment is wrong. Assigning it to pricePerMonth makes the annual plan render '$79.99/mo'. Fix: derive the per-month value from the annual price (price / 12, formatted in the same currency) instead of reusing priceString — $79.99/yr -> $6.67/mo.
